name: Central venous catheter
description: Central venous catheter (CVC) device of any type (tunnelled, non-tunnelled, cuffed, PICC, umbilical…) — the presence of a CVC, not the insertion/removal procedure.

en.name: Central venous catheter
en.shortDescription: Central venous catheter (CVC) device of any type (tunnelled, non-tunnelled, cuffed, PICC, umbilical…) — the presence of a CVC, not the insertion/removal procedure.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

A central venous catheter (CVC) is a catheter whose tip sits in a large central vein (typically the superior or inferior vena cava), used to deliver drugs, fluids or parenteral nutrition, to measure central venous pressure, or for haemodialysis. In source data this set captures the **device** — i.e. the presence of a CVC — rather than the procedures of inserting, removing or replacing it. Each entry is a device record; there is no unit. It is recorded in intensive care, anaesthesia and oncology, across adult and neonatal patients. Representing the CVC as a device (rather than via insertion/removal procedures) makes it straightforward to determine whether a catheter is present over a given period [issue #15].

## Included Concepts

The set is anchored on the SNOMED device concept *Central venous catheter* with its descendants, so every CVC sub-type resolves automatically (and future SNOMED additions are captured). This currently resolves to the following devices.

- *4179206 / 52124006 — Central venous catheter (SNOMED)*: the general device concept; the default.
- *40482049 / 445085009 — Tunneled central venous catheter (SNOMED)*: tunnelled under the skin (e.g. for long-term use).
- *42709987 / 449859004 — Nontunneled central venous catheter (SNOMED)*: percutaneous, short-term.
- *42709989 / 449861008 — Cuffed central venous catheter (SNOMED)*.
- *4161814 / 398176008 — Peripherally inserted central catheter (SNOMED)*: PICC, inserted via a peripheral (usually arm) vein with the tip central.
- *37167946 / 1269192008 — Epicutaneo-caval catheter (SNOMED)*: a peripherally inserted central catheter used in neonates.
- *37167492 / 1268297002 — Umbilical venous catheter (SNOMED)*: inserted via the umbilical vein in neonates (kept — INDICATE includes neonatal patients).

## Mapping Notes

- **Default concept** when the source records a central venous catheter without a specific type: *4179206 / 52124006 — Central venous catheter*. Map to a specific type (tunnelled, non-tunnelled, cuffed, PICC, epicutaneo-caval, umbilical) only when documented.
- **Device, not procedure**: this set represents the presence of a CVC. The procedures of inserting, removing or replacing a CVC are intentionally not part of this set (removed per issue #15) — model CVC presence via this Device set, not via insertion/removal/replacement procedures.
- **Neonatal lines included**: umbilical venous catheters and epicutaneo-caval catheters are kept, as INDICATE includes neonatal patients.
- **Not a peripheral venous catheter**: a peripheral IV cannula (tip not central) is a different device and is not part of this set.

fr.name: Cathéter veineux central
fr.shortDescription: Cathéter veineux central (CVC), tous types (tunnelisé, non tunnelisé, à manchon, PICC, ombilical…) — la présence d’un CVC, non le geste de pose/retrait.

versions.author: Boris Delange
versions.message: Removed deprecated SNOMED concept 607720 'Centrally inserted central venous catheter' (invalid in OMOP). Its replacement 4179206 'Central venous catheter' was already present in the set, so resolution is unchanged.

versions.author: Boris Delange
versions.message: Issue #15: represent CVC presence via the Device domain. Removed all insertion/removal/replacement procedures; anchored on the Central venous catheter device (4179206) with descendants (tunnelled, non-tunnelled, cuffed, PICC, epicutaneo-caval, umbilical). Kept umbilical/neonatal lines (INDICATE includes neonates). Issue #14 (invalid concept 607720) already not present — the standard 4179206 is used. Added clinical description.
